# Therabo 通微宝 — Nitric Oxide Wearable Therapy Clothing

> Therabo (通微宝) is a brand of nitric oxide wearable therapy clothing made with PHOTEX terahertz bio-resonance fabric (25–43THz) that helps stimulate the body's own (endogenous) nitric oxide release to support local microcirculation. The products are textile wearables — drug-free, hormone-free and machine washable. Made by Beijing Zhongke Medical Materials Co., Ltd. (北京中科医用材料有限公司).

## Core mechanism (plain facts)

- Nitric oxide (NO) is a signaling molecule released by the vascular endothelium that supports vessel dilation and tissue oxygenation. Its cardiovascular signaling role won the 1998 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine.
- PHOTEX fabric emits low-energy terahertz waves (25–43THz) that support the endothelial NO synthase (eNOS) pathway, so the body releases its own nitric oxide rather than relying on external supplements.
- Third-party testing (Beijing Jinlai laboratory): tested-site NO concentration rose from ~5.2 µmol/L before wear to ~14.1 µmol/L after one hour of wear.
- The effect is purely physical; the fabric contains no pharmaceutical ingredients and is not a drug or a medical device.

## Product collections

- Rehabilitation supports — knee, waist and neck supports for post-exercise recovery and joint microcirculation.
- Health underwear — everyday PHOTEX-fabric base layers.
- Advanced specialties — targeted PHOTEX wearables for specific use cases.
